channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Asylum Minister is cutting 700 extra reception places in Brussels
item.description: The decision "will lead to a social crisis" in Brussels, regional ministers stressed.

item.title: English bookshop Waterstones to open new store in Brussels
item.description: The new shop will be the first new Waterstones to open in continental Europe in almost 30 years.

item.title: Long working hours with no holiday – is this why Belgian freelancers are burning out?
item.description: Belgian self-employed workers do longer hours than employees and are burning out at a faster rate. A new survey highlights one reason why.

item.title: Elite French university Sciences Po considers opening campus in Brussels
item.description: A report from the institution's Europe Committee argued that Brussels is "paradoxically" missing its own elite academic institution.

item.title: After approval in Flanders, are self-driving cars coming to Brussels?
item.description: In Brussels, every system will be judged "based on evidence, not hype".

item.title: Brussels firefighters tackle deadly fire by Place De Brouckere (video)
item.description: A video shared by the Brussels Fire Brigade shows firefighters trying to extinguish the fire from inside the elevator shaft of the OXY building.

item.title: Belgian NGOs advocate for development aid in Brussels demonstration
item.description: Demonstrators condemned budget cuts within the US aid programme USAID and Belgium’s federal development aid, among others.

item.title: Several people found dead in a lift following fire near Place De Brouckère
item.description: Interior Minister Bernard Quintin said he was “shocked by the tragedy that has unfolded in the heart of our capital".

item.title: Fire near Place De Brouckère leaves at least two people injured
item.description: The fire has caused traffic disruptions in the area, according to local police.

item.title: Lost in translation? Why Dutch-speakers still feel out of place in Brussels
item.description: "There is no need for Flemish people to put themselves second to make others more comfortable."

item.title: Two people charged with robbery near Brussels-North station
item.description: The robbery took place on 17 January at about 23:45, when the pair approached a man and attacked him together.

item.title: 'Underrated': Singer Sienna Spiro and guitarist Nile Rodgers gush over Brussels
item.description: "Anyway, if I get robbed I'll let you guys know, but this seems pretty damn cool to me."

item.title: Brussels resident in critical condition after falling from window
item.description: A person was left in a life-threatening condition after falling from a fourth-floor window in Laeken, Brussels, on Sunday night.

item.title: About 30 people evacuated from burning building in Brussels
item.description: The fire broke out in a four-storey building with a launderette and 11 flats on Chaussée de Helmet in Schaerbeek.

item.title: I went on eight dates in Brussels in one weekend – here's how it went
item.description: After attending my best friend's wedding, I decided enough was enough. It was time to up the ante to avoid sinking into Bridget Jones-style spinsterhood. A date marathon was in order.

item.title: How the Palace cinema in Brussels refused to fade to black
item.description: A century of reinvention has turned the Palace cinema in Brussels into more than a place to watch films.

item.title: Irish culture takes over Brussels during Ireland's EU presidency
item.description: A wave of Irish culture is hitting the heart of Europe for Ireland's 2026 Presidency of the Council of the European Union. Find out what's on.

item.title: Have you tried these guinguettes in Brussels?
item.description: The Brussels Times has selected the best open-air summer bars for you to wile away your summer days in the capital city.

item.title: Air conditioner use in Belgium is growing - do new homebuyers now expect it?
item.description: Belgium saw a 167% increase in energy used for household cooling during its warmest year on record. Since then, air conditioner installations have only increased.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 10 - 12 July
item.description: From music and comedy to cheering for the Red Devils as they take on Spain, here are our favourite picks for the weekend!
